_schema: SOC Framework shadow_mode production policy v1
_comment: Actions listed in 'production_allowed' have shadow_mode: false in SOCFrameworkActions_V3
and are explicitly approved to execute in full mode during PoV and production.
Every entry MUST have a 'reason' and a 'category'.
All C/E/R destructive actions (isolate, block, delete, reset, revoke, enable) must
remain shadow_mode: true and must NOT appear here until PS production handoff.
Actions listed in 'dynamic_actions' have their action name resolved at runtime from
a context key or playbook input and cannot be statically validated. Each entry must
name the playbook and explain why static resolution is not possible.
These are warnings in CI, not hard failures.

soc-get-email-events.reason: Read-only query -- retrieves email event history from vendor, no state change on any system.

soc-get-email-forensics.reason: Read-only query -- retrieves forensic metadata for an email message, no state change.

soc-file-exists.reason: Read-only check -- queries whether a file path exists on an endpoint, no modification.

soc-enrich-file.reason: Read-only enrichment -- hash and reputation lookup against threat intel, no state change.

soc-detonate-file.reason: Sandboxed execution -- file detonated in an isolated analysis environment, not on a production system.

SOC_Email_Spread_Evaluation_V3.reason: Action name is passed as a playbook input rather than hardcoded. Resolved action is soc-audit-inbox-rules which is listed in production_allowed above.

name: SOCFrameworkActions_V3
